sentence_reviews.text: Decision trees recursively partition feature space; random forests average decorrelated trees, and gradient boosting adds trees that correct earlier residual error.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA decision tree learning and ensemble learning topics; PML decision trees, random forests, and boosting topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sentence accurately synthesizes tree, forest, and boosting mechanics from cited textbook topics.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees is to connect Recursive partitioning & ensembling, Supervised learning to downstream capabilities including Recommendation, ranking & personalization, Tabular predictive analytics & decision support.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA decision-tree and ensemble learning; PML tree ensembles and supervised learning topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Recursive partitioning, ensembling, and supervised learning are supported; the downstream recommendation/ranking and decision-support list is graph synthesis.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML tree ensemble/supervised learning topics; no direct recommender or personalization evidence in packet citations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Tree ensembles are plausible for ranking/personalization, but the cited sources do not directly establish this downstream claim.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Tabular predictive analytics & decision support
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA and PML decision-tree / ensemble supervised prediction topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Tree ensembles are standard feature-vector/tabular predictive models; decision-support wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Recursive partitioning & ensembling, Supervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA/PML tree learning, ensemble learning, and model-selection context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dependencies are supported, but the limitation wording is generic and not directly cited.

sentence_reviews.text: Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Recursive partitioning & ensembling.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA decision-tree and ensemble learning; PML decision trees/random forests/boosting topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Recursive partitioning and ensembling directly describe the family.

sentence_reviews.text: Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA learning from examples / decision-tree learning; PML supervised tree models.
sentence_reviews.notes: These models are standard supervised predictors, though trees can also be used in some unsupervised variants.

sentence_reviews.text: Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Regression losses (MSE, MAE, robust).
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML regression trees / boosting regression topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Regression losses apply to regression tree/boosting variants, but classification trees use other criteria, so common strength is appropriate.

edge_reviews.notes: Recursive partitioning and ensembling are defining mechanisms for the node; composes/core is supported.
Decision trees, forests, and boosted trees are standard supervised models; trained_under/core is defensible for this node.
Regression losses are used for regression variants; common strength is supported but not universal.
